name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Agents that narrate their work are the best team players
articles.summary: Agents like Claude Codex and Codex run in your terminal. From that strategic vantage point they can wield system tools (awk, bash, curl, git, python) as well as MCP tools. Here’s how that looks in the terminal. ⏺ Bash(awk -F’T’ ‘/2026-07-23T05:/’...
articles.author: Jon Udell

articles.title: Your LLM can scrape the web — locally, without writing throwaway code
articles.summary: Every time you ask an AI assistant to "get data from this website", one of two things happens: it hallucinates the data, or it writes a one-off Python script you'll never run again. Both are wasteful. The model already knows where the data lives and how to select it — what's missing is a safe,...
articles.author: Iurii Panarin

articles.title: The MCP Marketplace Is Booming: Inside Remote OpenClaw's 13,000+ Server Ecosystem for AI Coding Agents
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard for connecting AI applications to external systems that has gone from a niche Anthropic experiment to a $97-million-monthly-download ecosystem in under two years. At the center of this explosion is Remote OpenClaw, a directory service claiming...

articles.title: MCP Deep Dive, Part 11: The Protocol Was Never the Blocker — Rolling MCP Across the Enterprise
articles.summary: There are already tens of thousands of MCP servers in the world. Getting even one of them into a real company still tends to mean per-user OAuth, a consent screen, and an IT ticket — per connector, per employee. That's the actual blocker, and it was never the protocol. It's identity. This is Part...

articles.title: What it actually takes to post to 8 social networks
articles.summary: Someone asks you to add "share to social" to your product. You look at it and think: seven or eight REST calls, a week of work. articles.title: One REST API for 8 messaging channels: now with MCP access for AI agents
articles.summary: Every messaging channel has its own API, its own SDK, its own webhook format. Wire up WhatsApp, then SMS, then email, then Instagram… and you've built four integrations that all behave differently. articles.title: Bring Your App to the Agent

articles.title: Join us for our MCP Live! — A free livestream covering all things MCP
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is the open standard for connecting AI models to tools and data. It was first introduced to the world by Anthropic in November 2024, and is now the most widely adopted standard in the world of Generative AI. You can now use MCP servers to connect agents to your data...

articles.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
